text:
The parties shall jointly instruct [name of PODE] / [a suitable PODE, whose name shall be identified] as a single joint expert to provide a report in accordance with the attached letter of instruction, addressing:

the most cost-effective way to divide the pension provision available to [the applicant] / [the respondent] / [both parties] between the parties so as to provide equality of pension income [now] / [when the [applicant] / [respondent] reaches the age of [60] / [65]] / [when the parties reach their respective state pension age] / [other];

an estimate of the pension income that would be receivable by the [applicant] / [respondent] in each of the scenarios in paragraph i. above;

the most cost-effective way to divide the pension provision available to [the applicant] / [the respondent] / [both parties] between the parties so as to achieve equality of [capital value] / [CE] of those pensions;

[etc].

The identity of the expert[s] shall be [expert name[s]] / [chosen in accordance with paragraph [para number] above].

The letter of instruction [and [insert any other documents]] shall be sent to the PODE by [time and date].

The report shall be sent to the court in electronic format and served on the parties simultaneously by [time and date].

The costs charged by the PODE for preparing the report shall be met by the [applicant] / [respondent] / [parties equally] in the first instance.

[Any questions shall be put to the PODE by no later than 10 days after receipt of the report (in accordance with FPR 2010 r 25.10).]

[The PODE shall respond to those questions by [time and date].]

[The costs charged by the PODE for answering those questions shall be met by the [applicant] / [respondent] / [parties equally] / [party raising them] in the first instance.]

[Save as is expressly ordered by the court, the PODE’s written report shall be admissible without the attendance at court of the PODE.]

Taxation Issues

(see alternative versions in order 1.1)
